value.reason: The cited page explicitly documents multi-agent orchestration using the OpenAI Agents SDK with Codex CLI as an MCP server. It describes creating "a multi-agent team with hand-offs, guardrails, and full traces" — directly matching the capability of coordinating multiple agents that share work and communicate. The cell label "Multi-agent orchestration" and the description referencing an orchestrator agent breaking work into subtasks, handing off to specialized sub-agents via the Agents SDK, and routing results via handoffs are all faithfully supported by the page content. The page is not about context caching, subagents in isolation, workflows/saved automations, plan-then-execute mode, or parallel isolated workspaces specifically — it is about multi-agent teams with handoffs, which is exactly the capability being evaluated.
